posts.cooked:
Do you want edit your LBA2 saved games?

  • I want
  • I dont want
0 voters

I just want to know guys if you are interested in a program to edit the LBA2 saved games.

The editor wouldnt be like the El_Muerte LBASGE2, since I dont have all the information about the format because I just discovered few things. Things like, change the area code, view preview image, Inventory Items, Raining flag, Twinsens behavior, Kashes, Zlitos, Magic level, Magic power, Keys, Clover boxes, Clover leafs, Holomap locations, Twinsens position and angle.

Well, I probably will not discovered more than this things because will be really hard to do, but just one thing I need to find the Twinsen Life Points. Yeah its really weird but I cant find :geek:
Anyway I want to hear your sugestion!
- alexfont: And is it out yet? BTW, even all these things would be great!!! YAY!!!
I didnt make the program yet but the format with this things I told is ready. I first want to know if people would like an editor.

- alexfont: Of course!!! An LBA 2/TO savegame editor would be great!!! Anyway, if you want, you can just sand me the format for both games save games, and Ill make a super-dooper editor for both ones for you (of course, in VB 6). Interested?

Ill make myself, I just need time

Ill make the first version just compatible with LBA2 (the only needed for now) and maybe the next versions compatible for both games. The format Ill release when the programs comes out and Ill add too in the LBA File Info project.

Anyone have a suggestion for the program name?

Im asking just to not put the program with the same name as El_Muerte program (LBA Save Game Edit 2 aka LBASGE2).

LBASituEdit

(LBA Situation Editor)

Hmm I dont know, what do you think?
Dont know :). Will wait other suggestions
I suggest the following: LBADXSaveEd (LBA DeLuxe Save Editor).

OBrasilo:

Anyway, if you want, you can just send me the format for both games save games, and Ill make a super-dooper editor for both ones for you (of course, in VB 6). Interested?

You can get the file format now Enjoy
- alexfont: OK. So Ill make this editor. Just stay tuned.

Obras dont forget this one, otherwise Ill need to continue what I stopped the problem is the lack of time.

LBA2 Saved Game format.txt (27.3 KB)

- alexfont: I wont forget about it, I promise. Im only afraid it will be released only next year (as Im going to university in October, so I want have much time work on my stuff).

But I hope its not a problem to wait.

News, guys. I just resumed work on this program, and nearly all the required code is already written. So, stay tuned.

Edit: This screenshot was posted, like a year ago. Here you go:
.

Enjoy.

Finally OBras

Did you manage to find something else I didnt mention in the file format?

- alexfont: Yes, I found the life points. Theyre at offset +25E.

BTW, Im having a problem. The compressed saved game dont load properly. I mean, when I load an uncompressed saved game, the values are all normal, but when I load a compressed one, I get crazy values, such as 231 keys (whereas the game shows none with that particular saved game), etc. (and yes, I do uncompress the saved game).
Is there any change in the format if the saved game is compressed?

Yes, in the first byte:

00: byte Savegame identify, 0xA4 (compressed) or 0x24 (not compressed)

I had some program test here which loads the images and it work well ;), so you wont have any problem. It uses the mode 1 for decompress.
- alexfont: I know that, and from what I see, the image and the inventory items decompress well, but everything after seems to be screwed up.
Your probably missing a byte or something.

- alexfont: But then why do the uncompressed saved games load correctly?
I have a theory about that anyway. Maybe the general saved game format is somehow a bit different from the AUTOSAVE.LBA/CURRENT.LBA format.
